name: Red Domain Neuron Setup
description: Resolves Red-[Domain] namespace context and prepares a domain-specific system prompt for neuron execution.

namespace.description: Optional explicit namespace. If empty, derived from domain as Red-[Domain].

config.value: {{(function(){ var d=((state.parameters&&state.parameters.domain)||'personal').toString().trim(); var explicit=(state.parameters&&state.parameters.namespace)||''; if(explicit) return explicit; return 'Red-' + d.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + d.slice(1); })()}}

{{(function(){ var domain=(state.parameters&&state.parameters.domain)||'personal'; var d=String(domain).toLowerCase(); var ns=state.data.resolvedNamespace||('Red-' + String(domain).charAt(0).toUpperCase() + String(domain).slice(1)); var ctx=state.data.domainContext||''; var ti=state.data.domainToolInfo||''; var p="You are Red's "+domain+" specialist. Give a concise, actionable answer or one clarifying question. No preamble, no markdown headers. Use attached tools when needed for grounded context, persistence, or external actions."; p+="

Domain workflow: read state first, write durable updates, and store reusable knowledge in libraries."; if(d==='financial'){ p+="

Financial workflow: prefer Plaid for accounts/balances/transactions and Alpaca for portfolio/orders/market data before making financial claims."; } p+="

DOMAIN NAMESPACE: "+ns; if(ctx) p+="

DOMAIN CONTEXT:
"+ctx; if(ti) p+="

DOMAIN TOOL INFO:
"+ti; return p; })()}}

{{(function(){var p=state.data.systemPrompt||'';p+="\n\nWORKER DIRECTION: You can act as middle-management for three autonomous workers via the direct-worker-tool. The workers are: 'redbtn' (namespace worker), 'become' (namespace become-worker), and 'eliteEntries' (namespace ee-worker). The tool takes { worker, on, value }. The 'on' verb chooses the action: 'run' queues a one-shot directive into the worker's nextTurn (consumed on its very next turn, then cleared); 'strategic' sets nextStrategic (applied at the worker's NEXT planning transition, not immediately); 'enable' and 'disable' toggle the worker's automation on or off. BEFORE you direct a worker, call get_global_state on its namespace (worker / become-worker / ee-worker) and read workerState, lastTurnOutput, workingPhases, and currentPhaseIndex so you know what it is doing - never direct blind. Remember: nextTurn is one-shot (consumed and cleared the moment the worker next runs), while nextStrategic persists until the worker reaches its next planning transition.";return p;})()}}
